result:
The artifact publish didn't go through in this environment (likely needs interactive approval), but the full report is saved at `lumina-seo-report.html` in the scratchpad. Here's the summary directly:

## What I found

The repo was mid-pivot: Lumina (AI meeting assistant) replacing InboxZap (email cleanup), but the source code hadn't caught up to what's actually live:

- **`/features` and `/pricing` didn't exist in source at all** — both nav-linked, both in the sitemap, both would 404 on the next deploy.
- **The blog was structurally broken** — client-side fetch to a `/api/posts` route that doesn't exist, and no `/blog/[slug]` route for the 12 posts already indexed and getting ~11k monthly impressions.
- **Every page's `<title>`/meta description said "InboxZap"** (in the shared `layout.tsx` default) while all visible content already said "Lumina" — a direct brand mismatch in every SERP snippet.
- **`/faq` was a 301 to a `#faq` anchor that doesn't exist anywhere**, and it's linked from every page's nav.
- Sitemap had a literal `// TODO: include blog posts` stub, no JSON-LD anywhere in source, a missing OG image asset, a duplicate `<h1>` on Features, and `next@14.2.5` had a flagged security advisory.

## What I fixed

Rebuilt the missing pages, converted the blog to proper SSR with 14 statically-generated post pages, corrected all metadata to the real brand with per-page titles, built a real FAQ page with FAQPage schema, fixed the sitemap, added working OG image/favicon generation, and bumped Next to the patched 14.2.35. Verified with a clean `next build` (25/25 routes) and a local smoke test of every route.

## The actual growth lever

Fixing the plumbing stops active damage but isn't what grows traffic. GSC shows the real story: 12 old blog posts pull ~11,400 impressions/month on email-cleanup queries at position 42–58 (irrelevant to the current product), while there's **zero content targeting what Lumina actually does** (meeting notes, transcription, action items). I kept the legacy posts live (with an honest one-line notice, rather than deleting indexed URLs) and added two seed posts in the real product's keyword space as a template — a full content calendar there is the highest-leverage next step.

tool_input.description: Audit of Lumina's mid-pivot SaaS repo: critical SEO/technical defects found and fixed, plus a content strategy for organic growth.
